```{r eval=FALSE}
# this is the code to compute random combinations to be added to dfs. The values are precomputed and previsoulsy loaded.
combs_length <- c(30, 2, 3, 4, 5)
random_combs <- c(0,0,0,0,0)
set.seed(1492)
for (i in combs_length){
     random_list <- sample(rownames(hd), i)
     df <- signature_score(hd, random_list)
     dfs[['pbmcCovid']][[paste0('random_', as.character(i))]] <- df$scaled_combined_score
     random_combs[which(combs_length==i)] <- paste0('random_', as.character(i))
}
```


```{r fig.height=7, fig.width=9}
p1 <- ggplot(dfs[[2]], aes(x = cell_type, y = random_2, fill = cell_type)) + 
        geom_boxplot(outlier.shape= NA, show.legend = FALSE) + 
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)) + xlab("") 
p2 <- ggplot(dfs[[2]], aes(x = cell_type, y = random_3, fill = cell_type)) + 
        geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, show.legend = FALSE) + 
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)) + xlab("") 
p3 <- ggplot(dfs[[2]], aes(x = cell_type, y = random_4, fill = cell_type)) + 
        geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, show.legend = FALSE) + 
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)) + xlab("") 
p4 <- ggplot(dfs[[2]], aes(x = cell_type, y = random_5, fill = cell_type)) + 
        geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, show.legend = FALSE) + 
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)) + xlab("") 
plot_grid(p1, p2, p3, p4, align = "h", nrow = 2, rel_widths = c(0.5, 0.5))
```

```{r fig.height=5, fig.width=9}
p5 <- ggplot(dfs[[2]], aes(x = cell_type, y = random_30, fill = cell_type)) + 
        geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, show.legend = FALSE) + 
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)) + xlab("") 
p5
```


Here random combinations do not have a preferential cluster and show a generalized higher background signal noise (same happens for PBMC-3K dataset, not shown).  

Beyond using scores of random combinations as negative control, they also show the magnitude of background noise associated to each random combination taken into consideration. In fact, random scores show quite homogeneously distributed values across clusters indicating that the cluster-preferential distributions of real combinations scores are not stochastic.
Generally speaking since the higher is the length of random signatures, the higher will be the noise hence the probability to pick genes expressed in all the clusters: this suggests that the higher is the length of a real DEG signature the higher is the probability to include less specific genes and to increase the noise.

## Conclusions

This guided tutorial shows how to use the combiroc package to optimize single-cell gene signature with combinations of marker and to identify NK cells in two different public scRNAseq datasets:   

- combiroc accurately predicts NK cells already at default values without fine-tuning parameters, but the performances further improved incrementing the analysis' stringency.  
- combiroc's specificity can also ameliorate results obtained by other published gene-signature score: the gene-signature summarizing method is remarkably less noisy when performed on markers combinations compared to whole gene signatures.

Identifying NK cells using the expression level of the best markers individually is not efficient since they are too unspecific for classification purposes if taken one by one. The power of a combined signature of three or four markers only is comparable - and in some cases superior - to that of a whole scRNAseq gene signature, tipically in the range of tens of genes: __scaling down a gene signature while retaining its discriminatory power is highly relevant in both research and diagnostic context__.  

Finally, the usefulness of a highly specific tool, such as combiroc in the *"Sniper"* mode, is of particular relevance in cases in which researchers need to manually annotate cell population clusters obtained in scRNAseq experiments.  

Back to the [top](#top) of this doc

```{r}
sessionInfo()
```
